_comment: Every line below describes something that was actually observed on two real phones (SM-S928B + SM-G998U) driven over adb, with the on-chain effect verified against Solana devnet. Nothing here is aspirational — if a claim could not be demonstrated it is not in this script.

segments.text: Kickpact is a World Cup prediction app on Solana. Friends put the same stake on a match, and the pot is settled by TxLINE's cryptographic proof of what actually happened — not by us.
It ships as a real Android app, a desktop build, and a web dashboard. Everything you're about to see runs on two physical phones against Solana devnet.

Signing in is self-custodial, and there are three ways in. Privy — an email or a social account, which mints you an embedded Solana wallet. Phantom or Solflare, over Mobile Wallet Adapter. Or a burner key sealed in the device keychain, which is what these two phones are running on.
Both phones mint test dollars from the on-chain faucet. Watch the balances and the gas — these are real devnet transactions, confirmed before the screen updates.
This is the live TxLINE feed: World Cup fixtures, StablePrice odds, and the implied probabilities. Spain three twenty-five, the draw two-oh-six, Argentina four eighty.
The first phone opens a pool and takes Argentina. Ten test dollars leave the wallet and land in an escrow that no admin key can touch.
The second phone — a different device, a different wallet — sees that same pool appear, takes Spain, and matches the stake. Twenty dollars are now escrowed on chain, on opposite sides.
Now the part that needs two real radios. Both phones open the nearby room and find each other over Bluetooth, using Google Nearby Connections. No server, no internet — just proximity.
They connect, and the room goes live with two people in it. A message typed on one phone arrives on the other over the Bluetooth link.
segments.note: verified: 'CUM3HB: argentina taking it 1' delivered A<->B

segments.text: Then a duel is opened right there in the room. The invite travels over Bluetooth — but the money never does. The stake goes straight into the same on-chain escrow, and the friend joins the pot from their own phone.
Both phones show the same receipts, because both are reading the same chain. Every pool, every pot, every settlement.

Here is a settled one. England one, Argentina two. The program did not take our word for it — it was handed TxLINE's Merkle proof of both final goal counts, rebuilt the winning condition itself, and called into TxLINE's oracle program to check it.
And you can re-run that check from the phone, right now, against the live oracle on devnet. Oracle confirms. That is the whole point: the data decides, and anyone can audit it.

Bluetooth duels with the people next to you, code duels with the people who aren't, one escrow primitive underneath all of it, and settlement that traces back to a proof. Kickpact — built on TxLINE, settled on Solana.
